sections.content:
The Coordinate Plane

The coordinate plane has two axes: the horizontal x-axis and the vertical y-axis. They meet at the origin (0, 0). A point is described as (x, y) — always x first (horizontal), then y (vertical).

The four quadrants:
• Quadrant I: x > 0, y > 0 (top right)
• Quadrant II: x < 0, y > 0 (top left)
• Quadrant III: x < 0, y < 0 (bottom left)
• Quadrant IV: x > 0, y < 0 (bottom right)

🔑 Equation of a straight line: y = mx + c
• m = gradient (slope) — how steep the line is
• c = y-intercept — where the line crosses the y-axis
Gradient = rise ÷ run = (y₂ − y₁) ÷ (x₂ − x₁)

Gradient and y-intercept

The gradient tells you how much y changes for each unit increase in x. A positive gradient goes up from left to right; negative gradient goes down.

Examples from y = mx + c:
• y = 3x + 2: gradient = 3, y-intercept = 2
• y = −½x + 4: gradient = −½, y-intercept = 4
• y = 5: gradient = 0 (horizontal line), y-intercept = 5
• x = 3: vertical line (gradient is undefined)

To find gradient from two points (1, 3) and (4, 9):
m = (9 − 3) ÷ (4 − 1) = 6 ÷ 3 = 2

Graphing and Interpreting Lines

To draw a straight line from its equation:
1. Find the y-intercept (set x = 0)
2. Use the gradient to find another point
3. Draw a line through both points

Parallel lines have the same gradient. Perpendicular lines have gradients that multiply to −1 (they are negative reciprocals: if m₁ = 2, then m₂ = −½).

To find the equation of a line through (2, 5) with gradient 3:
y = 3x + c → substitute: 5 = 3(2) + c → c = −1 → y = 3x − 1

questions.question: What is the gradient of the line y = −3x + 7?
questions.explanation: In y = mx + c, m is the gradient. Here m = −3.

questions.question: Find the gradient between points (2, 1) and (6, 9)
Which equation represents a line parallel to y = 4x − 1?
questions.explanation: Parallel lines have the same gradient. y = 4x + 7 has the same gradient of 4.

questions.question: What is the y-intercept of the line 2y = 6x − 10?
Find the equation of a line with gradient 2 passing through (0, −3)
questions.explanation: Using y = mx + c with m = 2 and y-intercept c = −3: y = 2x − 3.
